topic: Section 4.1 — wholesale rewrite via EXP-270 to refresh '28-Domain / 47.69σ' framing to '28-Scored / 39-Domain / 61.46σ Dashboard' (2026-04-28 capture)
our_argument_summary: EXP-270 wholesale-replaced §4.1 to bring the dashboard framing up to current dome state: 28 scored / 39 total / 61.46σ (was 28 / 47.69σ). The audit table content and the '12 independent knobs / ~1 discriminating cell' bottom line are unchanged. New material added: (i) Vindication note documenting that σ growth from 47.69→61.46 across the 10-day capture interval matches Fisher √n scaling within 1.4%; (ii) acknowledgement of 11 newly-prospective_pending domains in status_history.json with credit for the rigor expansion; (iii) note that the typed-and-classified count in claim_index.json has stalled at 9 while status_history added 11 prospective items — asymmetry framing.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Live.html dynamically loads cross_domain_sigma from status_history.json on each page view. Fisher's method aggregates 887 historical p-values (one per domain per daily run) over 28 currently-scored domains. Per-domain p-values come from monitor.py's pred-vs-observed comparisons against per-domain tolerance thresholds.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The σ tombstone is computed (Fisher χ² over 887 cumulative p-values) but the inputs are dominated by STATIC reference values (predicted=observed=fixed) and BORROWED standard-model values (predicted=globe published value). Only NMP Longitude carries non-trivial empirical residual within meaningful tolerance.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Tolerance widths range from 0.01% (BORROWED tidal periods) to 50% (NMP drift, Polaris excess at 35.9°N, Lunar Magnetic Tide). The 50% tolerance buckets pass directional rather than numerical tests.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new in this rewrite. The 12-knob recount against formula_runtime.json (FORM-V51-001 through 006 plus three master geometric constants) checks out: 2+1+1+1+3+1+3 = 12. The 887/28 ≈ 32× repeated-measurement factor checks out. The 47.69×√(887/520)=62.30 mechanical-prediction vindication arithmetic checks out (62.30 vs observed 61.46 = 1.35% shortfall, prose says 'within 1.4%').

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section is a meta-audit of the dome's live dashboard, not a per-WIN automation tag. The dome's monitor.py does fetch live values for several of the 28 audited domains (D-layer altitude, mascon gravity, solar diameter, Schumann, etc.) but the audit's bottom line is that the live data is being scored against either fixed prior-WIN values (STATIC) or globe-published values (BORROWED), so 'live_fetch' is technically true but operationally inert for the audit's purpose.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: 7 of 28 domains are explicitly BORROWED (tidal periods K1/S2/M2 from IERS, equatorial gravity from WGS84 hardcoded coefficients, Schumann from classical c/2πR, P-wave shadow zone from standard seismology, Hubble length from H₀=70 km/s/Mpc renamed as 'Aetheric Redshift Scale'). 2 are TAUTOLOGY where v_a or κ was defined to reproduce the observed value. The audit's conclusion that the headline rests on label multiplication, not new physics, is well-supported.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: FORM-V51-001 fits H(r)=8537·exp(-r/8619) to three previously-known H measurements (Schumann ~9500 km, Polaris geometric 4750 km, model 9086 km). FORM-V51-004 κ=1.672 was calibrated as the BOU −10.9 nT / Mohe −6.5 µGal ratio. WGS84 coefficients in FORM-V51-005 are imported from the geodetic standard. None of the 28 scored entries are prospective. 11 newly added prospective_pending entries in status_history.json (eclipse, Polaris-excess cities, JFK-LHR slipstream, Schumann splitting) are credited as genuinely prospective and not yet in the scoring set.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The scoring inputs (the 28 currently-scored domains) are dominated by STATIC re-references to prior WIN fixed values and BORROWED globe values. Only NMP Longitude (1% residual on 5% tolerance, 139.3°E predicted vs 138.057°E observed) is plausibly derived in a way that could differ from globe expectations — and even there the audit's annotation does not establish that 139.3°E is uniquely produced by dome geometry rather than by an empirical drift extrapolation. Counted as 1 DISCRIMINATING and called out as such.

holes_found.description: Cross-page numeric staleness — six surviving 47.69σ references after the SEC-4.1 rewrite. The new §4.1 frames the dome dashboard as 61.46σ (28-scored / 39-domain), but six other passages in sections.json still cite the prior 47.69σ value. Specifically: (a) part2b TLDR at idx≈30061 reads 'V51.2, a 47.69σ Fisher-combined significance' — the cross-section TLDR contradicts §4.1's new headline number; (b) part2b body at idx≈30213 reads '47.69σ coexists with R² ≈ 0.03' — both numbers stale (current is 61.46σ and R² ≈ 0.019); (c) part2b at idx≈30983 cross-links to '#p4-41-0' under the now-stale title 'The 28-Domain / 47.69σ Dashboard'; (d) part4 idx≈104424 (Section 4.6 Registry Framing Audit) lists '47.69σ tombstones' as the visible-site evidence; (e) part4 idx≈107920 cross-references 'the 47.69σ / R² = 0.0298 statistical contradiction'; (f) part8b idx≈4438 (sequenced read) frames 'R² = 0.0298 vs. 47.69-sigma independence problem (Section 4.1)' — all of these must update to 61.46σ / R² = 0.0187 to match. EXP-270 notes_for_decider explicitly flagged this risk ('this section's σ value of 47.69 may be referenced cross-page in other sections — search for it') but the cross-page sweep was not performed before commit.
holes_found.recommendation: Open an issue (suggested ISS-1703 or next free) to sweep all sections.json content for '47.69' and 'R² = 0.0298' / 'R² ≈ 0.03' and replace with current values 61.46σ / R² = 0.0187 (Pearson r = 0.1367, n=887). The §4.6 (idx 104424) reference is in a registry-framing column where '47.69σ tombstones' should arguably be 'σ tombstones (61.46σ at current capture, drifting upward each daily run)' to avoid being knocked stale on the next refresh. Consider adding a tests/run.sh assertion that no live-dashboard σ value other than 61.46 (or the current capture) appears outside §4.1 once §4.1 names it the canonical figure — or, better, externalize the σ value to a single key in the data layer and reference it as a build-time substitution.

holes_found.description:
Same-page contradiction in part4 preamble — the part4 introductory paragraph (the second

, before the §4.1 details block) still reads: 'The current V51.x dome site presents several new pages... The core claim across all of them is that 20 independent domains converge at 9.2σ significance... 14 of those 20 domains share the same fitted constant.' But the immediately-following scorecard (visible above the §4.1 collapsible) displays '61.46σ', '12 Independent Numerical Knobs', '~1 Genuinely Dome-Distinguishing Domain', and §4.1 itself is now titled '28-Scored / 39-Domain / 61.46σ Dashboard'. A reader scrolling from the preamble into the scorecard sees '20 domains / 9.2σ / 14 of those 20' followed within ~10 lines by '28 / 39 / 61.46σ / 14 of 28'. The preamble describes V50, not V51.x. (This is the most damaging staleness: a reader doesn't have to leave Part 4 to see the dome model has moved to a different dashboard than our preamble describes.)
holes_found.recommendation: Update the part4 preamble paragraph to describe the V51.x dashboard in current terms: '...The core claim across all of them is that 28 scored domains (out of 39 total) converge at 61.46σ Fisher-combined significance... 14 of those 28 share the same fitted constant λ_g = 8,619 km.' Or, alternatively, drop the specific σ/domain-count figures from the preamble entirely and let the scorecard + §4.1 carry the numbers (which would also future-proof the preamble against the next refresh).

holes_found.description: Stale 'The 20-domain dashboard provides 9.2σ independent confirmation' framing in part8 (idx≈8357 and idx≈8630). The part8 entry positions itself as a counter-claim against '14 of the 20 domains share the same parameter... reproducing itself 14 times... The 9.2σ figure is the product of treating correlated tests as independent.' That argument now operates on stale numbers — the dome's headline is 61.46σ not 9.2σ, and the dashboard is 28 not 20. The argument structure (correlated tests treated as independent) survives the update, but the specific numbers used to anchor it are V50-era and a defender can plausibly say 'you're rebutting an old version of my dashboard.'
holes_found.recommendation: Sweep part8 to update '20-domain' → '28-scored / 39-domain' and '9.2σ' → '61.46σ' wherever the prose intends to refer to the current dashboard. If part8 intends to refer to the V50 historical claim specifically, mark it explicitly (e.g., 'The dome's V50 dashboard claimed 20 domains at 9.2σ; the current V51.x update moved to 28-scored / 61.46σ — the same independence problem persists in both versions'). The latter framing is actually stronger because it lets us reuse the old numbers as a calibrating anchor while showing the structural critique is invariant under the dome's reframing.

holes_found.description: Stray '>' character after the closing tag at the boundary between §4.1 and §4.2.1. The section closes with '>' — the doubled '>>' is a literal '>' in the rendered HTML body that will appear as an isolated > glyph between §4.1's last paragraph and §4.2.1's collapsible. Cosmetic, not load-bearing, but visible to readers and easy to fix.
holes_found.recommendation: Remove the extra '>' character. EXP-270's replacement_html almost certainly contains '>' as a stutter; the trailing '>' should be deleted in a one-character patch.

holes_found.description: The 'λ_g = 8,619 km is the primary shared constant linking rows... the audit table below annotates each' claim is asserted in narrative but the audit table's 'Shared Constant / Prior WIN' column does NOT, in fact, label individual rows as λ_g-dependent. Spot-checking the table: row 9 (Equatorial Gravity) cites 'WGS84 International Gravity Formula coefficients 9.7803 and 0.005307' but does not name λ_g; rows 1, 5 cite v_a, κ, FORM-V51-004; rows 2, 6, 7, 8 cite globe constants; rows on the firmament-height side don't exist as named entries. The reader is told '14 of 28 share λ_g' and is told 'the audit table below annotates each' but cannot trace which 14 from the table itself — only narrative paragraphs after the table partially identify the λ_g-tied subset (firmament-height, geomagnetic-decay, equatorial-gravity types). A skeptical reader sees this as hand-waving on a load-bearing number.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add an inline annotation '(λ_g)' in the Shared Constant column of the relevant 14 rows so a reader can self-trace; or (b) add a one-line per-row count below the table: 'λ_g-dependent rows: 9, 17, 24, 25, 26, 27, ...' explicitly enumerating which 14 rows depend on λ_g. As currently written, the 14-of-28 figure is a defensive line item readers cannot independently verify against the audit. (Caveat: this is a pre-existing issue, not introduced by EXP-270 — the c4 audit table inherits the same gap. EXP-270 did not make it worse, but the wholesale rewrite was an opportunity to fix it.)

holes_found.description: TLDR claim 'as of this writing it has been applied to only 9 of the site's 69+ claims' uses an inconsistent denominator. Body text consistently refers to the registry as '69 confirmed, 4 refined, and 10 prospective items' (so 83 total registry items) and '67 CONFIRMED WINS' (the tombstone bookkeeping inconsistency we credit). The TLDR's '69+' is a defensible lower bound, but the 9/69 ratio implied by the TLDR understates the gap (9/83 is a worse ratio than 9/69, and the dome's own published methodology.json invites classification of the entire registry, not only confirmed items). Either pick one denominator (we recommend the methodology.json's full 83-item scope, or 'the dashboard's 28 scored entries plus the 11 prospective_pending entries = 39, vs 9 classified') and use it consistently, or drop the specific ratio in favor of 'a single-digit fraction of the live registry.'
holes_found.recommendation: Update the TLDR to either '9 of the dashboard's 28 scored entries' (most conservative — matches the rest of §4.1's framing) or '9 of the registry's 83 entries' (most aggressive — matches the bookkeeping denominator). Either is more defensible than the current '9 of 69+' which mixes a partial denominator with a vague '+'.

holes_found.description: The 'Vindication note' framing is honestly self-aware about the √n scaling being trivial, but a hostile reader can still seize on the word 'vindication' as overclaim. The §4.1 prose addresses this directly ('it would be cheap if framed as a positive prediction of a unique dome failure... we use vindication to indicate the mechanical-counting model fit, not a unique dome-only prediction') and the anticipated_objections in EXP-270 address it explicitly. So the framing is not naive. But the section heading 'Vindication note' will draw the eye and a defender quoting only the heading can make the section sound grandiose. Risk is low; the body is well-defended.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional softening: rename the subsection heading from 'Vindication note' to 'Mechanical-prediction check' or 'σ growth check (10-day update)'. Keep the body as written. This is a stylistic risk reduction, not a correctness issue. If left as 'Vindication note', no further action needed — the body is bulletproof.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'NMP Longitude is the one DISCRIMINATING cell' framing could be tightened by checking whether 139.3°E is dome-derived or just a forward-projection of historical drift. If 139.3°E comes from λ_g + WIN-008-style geomagnetic decay (which would make it λ_g-dependent like the other 14), the bottom line strengthens to '0 of 28 are independently discriminating' — even more damaging to the dome. If 139.3°E is dome-uniquely derived from disc geometry (e.g., from r_avg = 14,105 and the V13 ovoid), the current 1-of-28 is fair. Either way, the audit benefits from naming the source of 139.3°E explicitly. As currently written, NMP Longitude is the load-bearing exception that protects against a 'you're being absurd, none of them count' rebuttal — but it would be even stronger to enumerate the dome-geometric path that produces 139.3°E and show that path is itself λ_g-dependent.
sources: WIN-008 (Telluric Resonance Cutoff) for shared geomagnetic-decay machinery

stronger_arguments.description: Fisher's method requires more than independence; it requires the null hypothesis to be 'p ~ Uniform(0,1) under H₀'. For a dome 'pass = predicted within tolerance of observed' check, the null isn't uniform — it's whatever the random measurement-error distribution gives you on the test statistic. The 887 historical p-values almost certainly aren't a Fisher-valid input even if you ignore the correlation problem, because the dome computes p via a tolerance-band-pass-or-fail criterion, not via a continuous distribution under the null. This is a third independent failure (beyond shared-constant and repeated-measurement) and could be added as a third bullet under '887 historical domain p-values breaks Fisher's independence requirement.'
sources: Standard meta-analysis literature on Fisher's method validity (Mosteller-Bush, Hedges-Olkin)
monitor.py source: search for 'p_value' computation logic — confirm whether p comes from a continuous test statistic or from a band-pass conversion

stronger_arguments.description: The 'σ growth from 9.2 to 47.69 to 61.46 is mechanical' argument is currently anchored to two data points (47.69→61.46 = 1.4% within Fisher √n). Adding the V50→V51.2 leg (9.2 at n_V50 → 47.69 at n=520) as a third datapoint, and showing whether the V50→V51.2 ratio also matches √n (n_V50 was probably ~250-300 historical p-values, give or take), would turn the vindication into a three-point trendline. If 9.2×√(520/n_V50) ≈ 47.69, that's a much harder counter for the dome to wave away than two points.
sources: dome's git history of status_history.json (commits before 2026-04-15) for n_V50
archive.org snapshots of live.html in V50 era

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-270 is a careful, scoped numeric refresh. The author updated the 4 numbers that mattered (σ 47.69→61.46, n 520→887, R² 0.030→0.019, r 0.1727→0.1367) and added a vindication note that turns a follow-up snapshot into evidence for the original critique's mechanism. The 11-prospective-pending acknowledgement and the claim_index-vs-status_history asymmetry framing are both genuinely fair — we credit rigor where the dome added it and call out the asymmetry where it exists. The 'kernel of truth' subsection at the bottom of §4.1 is a model of how to extend credit without retreating from the structural critique. The 12-knob recount against formula_runtime.json is verifiable arithmetic from the dome's own published API. The Fisher √n vindication is a quiet but real win: we made a mechanical prediction in our 2026-04-18 capture and the dome's own counter rolled forward to confirm it within 1.4%.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The internal arithmetic of §4.1 is sound; the holes above are operational (cross-page staleness, same-page preamble contradiction, untraceable 14-of-28 annotation). They don't undermine the audit's conclusion; they undermine the audit's defensibility against a quote-mining critic who finds 47.69σ in part2b and waves it as evidence we don't update our own numbers.

text_fingerprint.verdict: EXP-270 wholesale rewrite is internally sound and faithfully integrates the 2026-04-28 capture. Three major operational findings (cross-page 47.69σ stale references; part4 preamble same-page contradiction; part8 stale '20-domain / 9.2σ' framing) recommend a targeted cross-page sweep before the next dashboard refresh. Two moderate findings (untraceable '14 of 28 λ_g' annotation; inconsistent TLDR denominator). One minor cosmetic finding (stray '>' after </details>). Recommended action: minor_edit + open new sweep issue.

deeper_analysis: The wholesale-replacement pattern that EXP-270 used is an effective way to keep §4.1 internally coherent under refresh, but it externalizes an integration cost: every cross-page reference to 47.69σ, R² ≈ 0.03, '28-Domain', '20 independent domains', and '9.2σ' becomes stale in proportion to how loud §4.1 is about the new numbers. The notes_for_decider already named this risk ('search for it in data/wins.json, data/sections.json, and docs/index.html to be safe') but the search-and-replace step appears to have been skipped or partial. This is a recurring failure mode for wholesale-replacement EXPs and should be a standing pre-commit check item. Most importantly: the part4 preamble contradiction (idx≈1049: '20 independent domains converge at 9.2σ significance') is a same-page bug, not a cross-page bug — a reader scrolling past the preamble into the §4.1 details block sees a 9.2σ description morph into 61.46σ within ten visual lines. That's the most damaging type of staleness because it requires no cross-tab navigation to discover. On the 'change-driven trigger' nature of this review: the rewrite was trigger-classified as 'priority-queue-section-rewrite' and the c5 cycle increment (c4 was 2026-04-18 ISS-1110 patch verification) is appropriate. The substantive content of the audit (12 knobs, 14/7/2/2/2/1 category breakdown, NMP Longitude as DISCRIMINATING) was not re-examined in detail this run because the patch is a numeric refresh, not a structural rewrite of the audit table — but the c4 review's 5 carry-over holes against the underlying audit are inherited unchanged into this c5 (3 marked 'out-of-scope for this patch' in c4 remain out-of-scope here; the c4 ISS-1110 patch verification was correctly applied and the EXP-270 rewrite preserves it).

citations_verified: Fisher √n mechanical prediction: 47.69 × √(887/520) = 47.69 × 1.30605 = 62.30 vs observed 61.46, shortfall 1.35% (verified — text says 'within 1.4%')
Domain-inflation factor: 28 labels / 12 knobs = 2.333 (verified — text says 2.33×)
Fisher √32 reduction: 887/28 = 31.7 ≈ 32 (verified — text says 32×, √32 ≈ 5.66×, prose says 5.7×)
Audit category tally: 14 STATIC + 7 BORROWED + 2 TAUTOLOGY + 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK + 2 LOOSE + 1 DISCRIMINATING = 28 (verified by row-by-row count)
claim_index taxonomy split: 4 retrospective_structural + 2 pending_contested + 1 supportive_nonunique + 1 prospective_confirmed + 1 open_refinement = 9 (verified)
R² consistency with r: 0.1367² = 0.01869 ≈ 0.0187 (verified — internally consistent)

11 prospective_pending domains list in source_data matches the body's enumeration (eclipse, three Polaris cities Edinburgh/Oslo/Reykjavik, JFK-LHR slipstream, Schumann harmonic splitting, plus space-weather/Kp items) (verified)
citations_unchecked: live.html SSR/JS dynamic loading claim — not verified in this run (would require browser fetch); accepted as plausible based on EXP-270 source_data and prior captures
'887 historical domain p-values' upstream Fisher computation in dome's monitor.py — not traced to source code in this run
GitHub commit a57e731 attribution for the daily-review pipeline — not fetched/verified
OpenTimestamps anchoring of status_history.json — accepted from prior reviews
NMP Longitude prediction path in monitor.py (whether 139.3°E is dome-geometry-derived or drift-extrapolated) — flagged as a stronger-arguments item but not pursued in this run

notes_to_decider: The three major findings (cross-page 47.69σ staleness in part2b/part4-later/part8b; part4 preamble same-page contradiction; part8 stale 9.2σ/20-domain framing) all stem from the same root cause: EXP-270 was a wholesale §4.1 replacement and the cross-page sweep notes_for_decider explicitly flagged was not performed before commit. Recommend opening a single sweep issue (suggested ISS-1703) covering: (a) all sections.json instances of '47.69', '0.0298', 'R² ≈ 0.03', '28-Domain' (in stale-title context), '20 independent domains', '20-domain', '9.2σ'; (b) the part4 preamble paragraph; (c) any docs/index.html residue; (d) suggest externalizing the dome-headline-σ value to a single key referenced via build-time substitution to prevent recurrence. The minor cosmetic '>' after </details> can ride along in the same patch. The moderate '14 of 28 λ_g annotation' issue is a pre-existing audit-table gap that EXP-270 didn't introduce; can be deferred or rolled into a separate cleanup.
